lede: A look at September 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Precious Achiuwa and Renee Paquette, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Azerbaijan
events.desc: Azerbaijan launches a military offensive against the Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region; this leads to the flight of the Armenian population.

events.title: Mariano Rivera
events.desc: Mariano Rivera of the New York Yankees surpasses Trevor Hoffman to become Major League Baseball's all-time career saves leader with 602.

events.title: Industrial Society and Its Future
events.desc: The Washington Post and The New York Times publish the Unabomber Manifesto.

events.title: Ötzi
events.desc: Ötzi the Iceman is discovered in the Alps on the border between Italy and Austria.

events.title: 1985 Mexico City earthquake
events.desc: A strong earthquake kills thousands and destroys about 400 buildings in Mexico City.

events.title: Kostas Georgakis
events.desc: Kostas Georgakis, a Greek student of geology, sets himself ablaze in Matteotti Square in Genoa, Italy, as a protest against the dictatorial regime of Georgios Papadopoulos.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: An attack by North Korean forces was repelled at the Battle of Nam River.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Kępa Oksywska concludes, with Polish losses reaching roughly 14% of all the forces engaged.

events.title: Women's suffrage in New Zealand
events.desc: In New Zealand, the Electoral Act of 1893 is consented to by the governor, giving all women in New Zealand the right to vote.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Union troops under William Rosecrans defeat a Confederate force commanded by Sterling Price.

deaths.text: William Ungar, Polish-American author and philanthropist, founded the National Envelope Corporation
Einar Gerhardsen, Norwegian civil servant and politician, 1st Prime Minister of Norway
Vishnu Narayan Bhatkhande, Indian singer and musicologist
Ole Rømer, Danish astronomer and instrument maker

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alonso de Orozco Mena
Christian feast day: Januarius (Western Christianity) Feast of San Gennaro
Christian feast day: Our Lady of La Salette
Christian feast day: Theodore of Tarsus (Anglican Communion, Roman Catholic Church, Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Trophimus, Sabbatius, and Dorymedon
